channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: NVIDIA’s Own AI Costs Now Exceed What It Pays Human Employees, VP Admits, as Compute Bills Spiral Across the Industry
item.description: AI is great for many tasks, but the costs to run AI are also exponentially higher than those of human workers, says NVIDIA. NVIDIA Highlights Large Cost of Running AI Versus Human Employees, While Suggesting That Humanity's Future Revolves Around The Adoption… [Nvidia, ServiceNow]

item.title: Data centers: Critical tech infrastructure under threat
item.description: Data centers are energy-intensive engines of growth, the backbone and hub of digitalization. Thousands of them are being built all around the world. The Iran war has shown how vulnerable societies are as a result.

item.title: Paperwork causing AI mission lag as startups flag funding, IP concerns
item.description: IndiaAI mission startups face delays in formal agreements, impacting non-compute support and raising IP concerns. While compute access is provided, founders report operational hurdles and potential timeline shifts due to pending MoUs. The government is explor…

item.title: Amazon boosts CAPEX to $44.2B, up from $25B last year

item.title: Google’s free cash flow will hit zero next year
item.description: The commodities strike back Alphabet generated $73.266 billion in free cash flow in FY2025, which sounds enormous until you look at what’s coming. CEO Sundar Pichai has guided for 2026 capital expenditures in the range of $175 to $185 billion. For context, Al… [Alphabet]
